subject: [PATCH 3/3] fuse: end fuse_req on io-uring cancel task work

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author Joanne Koong disagreed with the implication that io_uring task death or ring death is equivalent to fuse connection death, explaining that a single worker thread exiting doesn't drop any reference on /dev/fuse fd.

Author acknowledged that the cancel branch needs to call fuse_uring_req_end() to prevent the application syscall thread from being stuck uninterruptibly in D-state, and agreed that this is necessary even for cases where tw.cancel occurs without a subsequent fuse abort.
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think tw.cancel implies a connection abort. tw.cancel gets set
on io_uring task death or ring death which is different from fuse
connection death (eg a single worker thread of a multithreaded daemon
exiting doesn't drop any ref on /dev/fuse fd, but that thread's
in-flight task work still drains with tw.cancel)

---

I think this is needed for the cases where tw.cancel occurs without a
subsequent fuse abort, else the application syscall thread is stuck
uninterruptibly in D-state in request_wait_answer() for the
connection's lifetime. tw.cancel with a fuse abort is the common case,
but I think unfortunately we also need to handle the case where this
doesn't occur.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: Reviewer Bernd Schubert noted that the initial code used IO_URING_F_TASK_DEAD, which he had incorrectly assumed was related to PF_EXITING, and suggested that instead of calling fuse_uring_req_end() directly in the cancel branch, the code should go through the existing teardown function fuse_uring_entry_teardown(),
reviews.raw_body:
I see, the initial code was using IO_URING_F_TASK_DEAD and I had wrongly 
assumed that is related to PF_EXITING. 
Well, I think the fix is clear, although I personally do not like the
exit code dup (or better triple)
https://lore.kernel.org/r/20260515045541.1171335-4-joannelkoong@gmail.com

In my option fuse_uring_cancel() and canceled fuse_uring_send_in_task() 
should go through fuse_uring_entry_teardown().


Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the cancel branch should free and clean up the ent immediately instead of keeping it around until the fuse connection gets aborted, but doesn't feel strongly about it and is willing to go with reviewer's preference.
sentiment_signals: happy to go with what you prefer
reviews.raw_body:
I don't feel strongly about this. On cancel, the ent becomes useless
since cancel consumes the cmd, so imo it makes sense to just free and
clean up the ent immediately instead of keeping it around until the
fuse connection gets eventually aborted, but I don't think it really
matters. Happy to go with what you prefer.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a need for minimal changes to ensure the patch can be backported to stable, promising a revised version (v2) that addresses this concern.
reviews.raw_body:
This fix (as well as [1]) needs to be backported to stable so I think
it'd be better to keep the fix as minimal as possible and do any
entry_teardown deduping as a follow-up cleanup after that. I'll send
out a v2 that fixes it minimally.

Thanks,
Joanne

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/fuse-devel/20260516021138.2759874-4-joannelkoong@gmail.com/

2026-06-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the io-uring implementation for FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) where a request is not properly ended when the task work is cancelled, leading to incorrect behavior and potential stalls. The fix involves calling fuse_uring_req_end() before falling through to fuse_uring_send() in the cancel branch of fuse_uring_send_in_task(). This ensures that the request is correctly ended and its reference is discharged, preventing issues such as repeated cancels inflating a counter and stalling background operations.
